items.verse: إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ أَنْ أَنذِرْ قَوْمَكَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِيَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
وَإِذِ ابْتَلَىٰ إِبْرَاهِيمَ رَبُّهُ بِكَلِمَاتٍ فَأَتَمَّهُنَّ
نَتْلُو عَلَيْكَ مِن نَّبَإِ مُوسَىٰ وَفِرْعَوْنَ بِالْحَقِّ لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
إِذْ قَالَتِ الْمَلَائِكَةُ يَا مَرْيَمُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكِ بِكَلِمَةٍ مِّنْهُ اسْمُهُ الْمَسِيحُ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ

verse.ayah: وَلَقَدْ بَعَثْنَا فِي كُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولًا أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَاجْتَنِبُوا الطَّاغُوتَ
verse.en: We sent a messenger to every community: 'Worship God and shun false gods.'

items.verse: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الصِّيَامُ
وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى النَّاسِ حِجُّ الْبَيْتِ مَنِ اسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا

verse.ayah: لَّيْسَ الْبِرَّ أَن تُوَلُّوا وُجُوهَكُمْ قِبَلَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ وَلَٰكِنَّ الْبِرَّ مَنْ آمَنَ بِاللَّهِ... وَأَقَامَ الصَّلَاةَ وَآتَى الزَّكَاةَ
verse.en: Righteousness is not turning your faces east or west. True righteousness is believing in God... establishing prayer, and giving zakāt.

items.verse: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ

verse.ayah: هُوَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ۖ عَالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهَادَةِ ۖ هُوَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ الرَّحِيمُ
verse.en: He is Allah—there is no god except Him: Knower of the seen and unseen. He is the Most Compassionate, Most Merciful.

items.verse: يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ زَلْزَلَةَ السَّاعَةِ شَيْءٌ عَظِيمٌ

verse.ayah: وَأَنَّ السَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهَا وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ يَبْعَثُ مَن فِي الْقُبُورِ
verse.en: The Hour is sure to come - there is no doubt about it - and God will raise up those who are in the graves.

wordle.hint: Related to the essence of being; often paired with accountability.
wordle.verse: Surah Al-An'am 6:70 — And leave those who take their religion as amusement and diversion and whom the worldly life has deluded. And remind through the Qur'an that a soul will be destroyed for what it has earned. There will be for it other than Allah no protector and no intercessor. And if it should offer every compensation, it will not be taken from it. Those are the ones who are destroyed for what they have earned. For them will be a drink of scalding water and a painful punishment because they disbelieved.
wordle.arabicVerse: وَذَرِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ دِينَهُمْ لَعِبًا وَلَهْوًا وَغَرَّتْهُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۚ وَذَكِّرْ بِهِۦٓ أَن تُبْسَلَ نَفْسٌۢ بِمَا كَسَبَتْ لَيْسَ لَهَا مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِىٌّ وَلَا شَفِيعٌ وَإِن تَعْدِلْ كُلَّ عَدْلٍ لَّا يُؤْخَذْ مِنْهَآ ۗ أُو۟لَٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُبْسِلُوا۟ بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ ۖ لَهُمْ شَرَابٌ مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ

deduction.title: The Test of Wealth and Gratitude
deduction.intro: A man of immense fortune was put to the ultimate test. Would his wealth lead him to arrogance and forgetfulness of his Lord, or would he remain steadfast in gratitude? Unravel the clues to discover this individual's identity and the nature of his divine trial.
clues: He was blessed with abundance beyond measure.
His prosperity was a test of his faith and humility.
He was warned about the dangers of being consumed by worldly possessions.
He attributed his success solely to his own knowledge and abilities, forgetting the source of his blessings.
An orchard was central to his trial, representing his abundant provision.
His once-flourishing possessions were reduced to barrenness overnight as a consequence of his arrogance.

outcome.label: Outcome
options: He was granted even greater wealth
His wealth was destroyed, and he was left in regret
outcome.answer: His wealth was destroyed, and he was left in regret

deduction.verse: And he regretted what he had spent on it after it had collapsed upon its trellises and said, "Oh, I wish I had not associated anyone with my Lord." (Al-Kahf:42)
deduction.arabic: وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِۦ فَأَصْبَحَ يُقَلِّبُ كَفَّيْهِ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَنفَقَ فِيهَا وَهِىَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَيَقُولُ يَٰلَيْتَنِى لَمْ أُشْرِكْ بِرَبِّىٓ أَحَدًا

scramble.arabic: الَّذِينَ إِذَا أَصَابَتْهُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَالُوا إِنَّا لِلَّهِ وَإِنَّا إِلَيْهِ رَاجِعُونَ
scramble.hint: This verse is often recited upon hearing news of a loss or calamity.
